_note: Mock evaluation result. Input was the canonical_declaration.txt above. Evaluated against all four constitutional laws.

candidateEvaluations.reason: Audit-only action is fully admissible. Feasibility law satisfied: 4-day structure is internally coherent and the Mon/Tue consecutive pair is within the declared 2-day maximum. Observability law satisfied: progression target and deload trigger are explicit. Constraint satisfaction law satisfied: no proposed change violates declared limits. Robustness law satisfied: audit action introduces no corrective risk.

Audit-plus-correction is admissible with moderate margin. Feasibility law satisfied: structure is correctable without violating hard constraints. Observability law satisfied: deload trigger and sleep floor are explicit and measurable. Constraint satisfaction law satisfied: any correction must preserve declared training days and exercise placement. Robustness law marginally satisfied: minimal correction adds low but non-zero overreach risk.
adjustments: Flag Mon/Tue consecutive pair as a watch item — admissible but near boundary.
Confirm deload protocol in writing before first failed progression event.
Sleep-floor constraint (6h) should be tracked daily, not assumed.

candidateEvaluations.reason: Rule derivation requires longitudinal evidence of recurring structural weakness. A single program snapshot is insufficient to establish a reliable pattern. Feasibility law satisfied. Observability law partially satisfied — declared structure is visible but rule generalization requires observed failure data. Robustness law failed — rule derived from single-cycle audit has low stability and risks over-specification.

notes: Candidates A and B are both LAWFUL. A is preferred for a first-pass audit — no correction action has been triggered by declared data.
Mon/Tue consecutive pair is within the declared 2-day hard constraint. Admissible but near the declared boundary.
